# S01: Budget Contract Restoration (Backend + iOS)

**Restored budget reliability by validating a Student-Budgets-backed backend contract and preserving iOS retry-visible budget loading/saving behavior with deterministic verifier coverage.**

## What Happened

S01 closed the budget reliability gap at the API/data boundary. The slice now provides authenticated `GET /api/student/budget`, `POST /api/student/budget`, and `GET /api/budget-summary` contracts backed by a dedicated `Student Budgets` worksheet and PH-month spend aggregation from `Transactions Log`. Across T01â€“T03, implementation and contract coverage were verified end-to-end: student/month upsert semantics, stable `monthly_limit` + `monthly_spend` payload fields, explicit 401/404/503/500 envelopes, malformed-row skip-with-warning behavior, and iOS endpoint/decoder/retry marker alignment. The slice also shipped/validated a phased one-command verifier (`scripts/verify-m008-s01.sh`) that localizes failures by phase (preflight/backend/ios/retry/static). During closeout in this Windows harness, canonical `rtk proxy bash ...` remained blocked by missing `/bin/bash`; the same verifier passed through explicit Git Bash invocation, and this execution-path decision was recorded for future agents.

## Verification

Executed all slice-plan verification checks and confirmed green status: `rtk proxy python -m pytest -q tests/test_verify_m008_s01_budget_contract.py`; `rtk proxy python -m pytest -q tests/test_verify_m008_s01_budget_contract.py -k "unauthorized or unavailable or malformed"`; `rtk proxy python -m pytest -q tests/test_verify_m008_s01_ios_budget_contract.py`; `rtk proxy python -m pytest -q tests/test_verify_m007_s04_budget_lostcard_behavior_contract.py`; `rtk proxy "C:\Program Files\Git\bin\bash.exe" scripts/verify-m008-s01.sh` (all verifier phases passed). Observability/diagnostic surfaces were confirmed via route-level contract/failure tests plus phased verifier output (including static marker checks and retry-visibility regression suite).

## Requirements Advanced

- R073 â€” Implemented and verified budget limit + spend loading via dedicated backend routes and Student Budgets persistence with iOS contract parity.
- R074 â€” Maintained explicit load/save failure channels and retry actions in iOS budget flow, guarded by regression and static contract checks.

## Requirements Validated

- R073 â€” `rtk proxy python -m pytest -q tests/test_verify_m008_s01_budget_contract.py`; `rtk proxy python -m pytest -q tests/test_verify_m008_s01_ios_budget_contract.py`; `rtk proxy "C:\Program Files\Git\bin\bash.exe" scripts/verify-m008-s01.sh` (all phases pass).
- R074 â€” `rtk proxy python -m pytest -q tests/test_verify_m007_s04_budget_lostcard_behavior_contract.py`; `rtk proxy python -m pytest -q tests/test_verify_m008_s01_budget_contract.py -k "unauthorized or unavailable or malformed"`; retry/static phases in `scripts/verify-m008-s01.sh` pass via Git Bash invocation.

## New Requirements Surfaced

None.

## Requirements Invalidated or Re-scoped

None.

## Deviations

No major backend/iOS feature rewrites were required during closeout because core S01 implementation was already present; work focused on verification hardening and host-shell execution adaptation. In this harness, `rtk proxy bash scripts/verify-m008-s01.sh` still fails before script startup due missing `/bin/bash`, so explicit Git Bash invocation was used for equivalent verification.

## Known Limitations

Windows hosts without `/bin/bash` cannot run `rtk proxy bash scripts/verify-m008-s01.sh` directly; use `rtk proxy "C:\Program Files\Git\bin\bash.exe" scripts/verify-m008-s01.sh` for parity. Final device-level UX acceptance remains scoped to S06.

## Follow-ups

S05/S06 should keep using the S01 verifier as a prerequisite for integrated acceptance and use explicit Git Bash invocation on Windows executors. S06 manual acceptance should include budget load/save failure-injection checks to confirm user-visible retry behavior remains intact in the final UX.

## Files Created/Modified

- `backend/api/api_server.py` â€” Added/restored Student Budgets helpers plus `/api/student/budget` and `/api/budget-summary` contract behavior with explicit status envelopes and PH-month spend aggregation.
- `tests/test_verify_m008_s01_budget_contract.py` â€” Added/maintained backend contract coverage for auth, upsert semantics, unavailable/error envelopes, and malformed-row handling.
- `tests/test_verify_m008_s01_ios_budget_contract.py` â€” Added/maintained iOS contract marker checks for budget endpoints and payload field compatibility.
- `tests/test_verify_m007_s04_budget_lostcard_behavior_contract.py` â€” Retained retry-visibility regression checks used as S01 guardrail evidence.
- `tests/conftest.py` â€” Normalized one layer of quoted pytest `-k` selectors for wrapper compatibility without changing test intent.
- `scripts/verify-m008-s01.sh` â€” Implemented/validated phased S01 verifier with preflight checks, actionable failure guidance, and high-risk static contract assertions.
- `.gsd/KNOWLEDGE.md` â€” Recorded non-obvious verification patterns for Windows shell execution and quoted pytest selector handling.
- `.gsd/DECISIONS.md` â€” Recorded closeout decision D103 for Windows `.sh` verifier execution fallback via explicit Git Bash path.

# S02: Full UX Rollback Baseline + Native Tab Bar

**Native iOS tab navigation now runs on `TabView` (floating stitch shell removed) with a phased rollback verifier that also protects budget, QR, and login contracts.**

## What Happened

S02 delivered the structural navigation rollback anchor for M008 by replacing `StitchTabShell`/`StitchTabItem` composition in `MainTabView` with native `TabView(selection:)` and four native tab items (`Home`, `History`, `Budget`, `Settings`). The change intentionally preserved existing auth/session-expiry behavior (`showSessionExpiredAlert`, `Sign In`, `authManager.clearAll()`) so rollback of chrome did not alter session boundary semantics. To make this slice executable and safe for downstream rollback work, the slice added a dedicated source-contract suite (`tests/test_verify_m008_s02_ios_rollback_contract.py`) and a phased harness (`scripts/verify-m008-s02.sh`) that validates shell migration and keeps budget, QR, and student-ID-only login regressions in the same closure command.

## Verification

Re-ran all slice contracts and phased checks successfully:
- `rtk proxy python -m py_compile tests/test_verify_m008_s02_ios_rollback_contract.py`
- `rtk proxy python -m pytest -q tests/test_verify_m008_s02_ios_rollback_contract.py`
- `rtk proxy python -m pytest -q tests/test_verify_m008_s01_ios_budget_contract.py`
- `rtk proxy python -m pytest -q tests/test_verify_m007_s02_qr_behavior_contract.py`
- `rtk proxy python -m pytest -q tests/test_verify_m007_s09_override_contract.py::test_login_state_and_payload_are_student_id_only`
- `rtk proxy "C:\Program Files\Git\bin\bash.exe" scripts/verify-m008-s02.sh`
All passed, including all verifier phases and final `status=passed`.

## Requirements Advanced

- R068 — Implemented native tab chrome in `MainTabView` and removed stitch-shell markers while preserving root app navigation semantics.
- R069 — Delivered native `TabView` + `.tabItem` shell replacement with explicit source-contract enforcement.
- R076 — Embedded QR continuity regression checks in the phased S02 verifier to ensure rollback work does not break existing QR-path contracts.

## Requirements Validated

- R069 — `tests/test_verify_m008_s02_ios_rollback_contract.py` and `scripts/verify-m008-s02.sh` pass with native tab markers present and stitch-shell markers absent.

## New Requirements Surfaced

- None.

## Requirements Invalidated or Re-scoped

None.

## Deviations

`rtk proxy bash scripts/verify-m008-s02.sh` is not runnable on this Windows host when `/bin/bash` is absent; verifier execution uses `rtk proxy "C:\Program Files\Git\bin\bash.exe" scripts/verify-m008-s02.sh` with identical script semantics.

## Known Limitations

S02 only establishes the navigation-shell rollback baseline and guards; broader pre-M007 surface rollback (home card-style hero, transactions filter-only UX, settings minimalism refinements) remains for subsequent slices. Swift LSP diagnostics are still unavailable on this host, so proof is contract-test/verifier driven.

## Follow-ups

S03 should proceed from this shell baseline and avoid reintroducing custom floating-tab abstractions. Keep `scripts/verify-m008-s02.sh` in the default pre-merge loop while Home rollback and QR continuity refinements land, so tab-shell drift is caught before later UX slices.

## Files Created/Modified

# S03: Home Rollback + Credit-Card Hero + QR Continuity

**Delivered a minimalist credit-card Home rollback while preserving QR success continuity seams and adding a phased S03 verifier that chains S02 regression guards.**

## What Happened

S03 completed in two steps: Home surface rollback and closure hardening. T01 simplified `HomeView` to a lightweight credit-card hero + recent activity surface, but explicitly kept the QR success seam intact (`didConsumePresentedQRSuccess`, continuity tick increment, and refresh handoff) so cross-screen continuity behavior does not regress during UI rollback. T01 also kept explicit failure recovery visible via an in-card retry path. T02 then hardened closure with `scripts/verify-m008-s03.sh`, a phased verifier that runs preflight, S03 Home rollback contract tests, the targeted M007 continuity-node regression, and finally chains S02 rollback/budget/QR/login guards. During closure we fixed a command-execution fragility in Windows automation by switching plan-level verifier invocation to `C:/Progra~1/Git/bin/bash.exe` (no-space path), which avoids quote-tokenization failures seen with `"C:\Program Files\..."` command strings.

## Verification

Executed all slice-level checks from the plan successfully:
- `rtk proxy python -m pytest -q tests/test_verify_m007_s07_integration_behavior_contract.py::test_qr_success_handoff_remains_wired_from_home_sheet_to_refresh_path` (pass)
- `rtk proxy python -m py_compile tests/test_verify_m008_s03_ios_home_rollback_contract.py && rtk proxy C:/Progra~1/Git/bin/bash.exe scripts/verify-m008-s03.sh` (pass)

`verify-m008-s03.sh` passed all phases, including chained `scripts/verify-m008-s02.sh` regression phases (`s02-rollback-contract`, `budget-regression`, `qr-regression`, `login-regression`).

## Requirements Advanced

- R070 — Implemented and contract-verified the Home credit-card hero rollback surface in `HomeView.swift` with S03 rollback marker checks.
- R076 — Preserved and re-verified the QR success continuity seam and chained continuity-node regression checks through the S03 verifier.
- R069 — Kept native TabView rollback guard enforcement active in S03 by chaining `scripts/verify-m008-s02.sh` inside S03 closure.

## Requirements Validated

None.

## New Requirements Surfaced

None.

## Requirements Invalidated or Re-scoped

None.

## Deviations

T01 introduced the initial S03 Home contract test earlier than originally split in plan sequencing (test creation landed before T02). Closure also hardened the T02/S03 plan verifier command to the no-space Git Bash short path for runner compatibility.

## Known Limitations

This slice proves source contracts and verifier-chain behavior only; it does not prove final on-device UX feel/performance acceptance for the Home rollback. Full integrated user-flow validation remains in downstream slices (S05/S06).

## Follow-ups

Carry the no-space Git Bash invocation pattern into downstream M008 plan-level verify commands to avoid command-tokenization failures in automation. In S04/S05, keep `scripts/verify-m008-s03.sh` as an upstream gate before declaring integrated rollback closure.

## Files Created/Modified

- `mobile/ios/BankongSetonStudent/Views/Home/HomeView.swift` — Rolled Home to minimalist credit-card hero while preserving QR success callback/tick refresh seam and explicit retry surface.

S03 completed in two steps: Home surface rollback and closure hardening. T01 simplified `HomeView` to a lightweight credit-card hero + recent activity surface, but explicitly kept the QR success seam intact (`didConsumePresentedQRSuccess`, continuity tick increment, and refresh handoff) so cross-screen continuity behavior does not regress during UI rollback. T01 also kept explicit failure recovery visible via an in-card retry path. T02 then hardened closure with `scripts/verify-m008-s03.sh`, a phased verifier that runs preflight, S03 Home rollback contract tests, the targeted M007 continuity-node regression, and finally chains S02 rollback/budget/QR/login guards. During closure we fixed a command-execution fragility in Windows automation by switching plan-level verifier invocation to `C:/Progra~1/Git/bin/bash.exe` (no-space path), which avoids quote-tokenization failures seen with `"C:\Program Files\..."` command strings.

Inbound socket event handlers do not gate on arduinoConnected
decisions.choice: completeNFCSale and card_read handlers call their fetch functions unconditionally; no arduinoConnected check
decisions.rationale: Connection-state gating is only meaningful for outbound actions (initiating a read); by the time nfc_payment fires, the tap already arrived — gating would silently drop a real payment event

decisions.decision: arduino_last_heartbeat initialized to 0.0 float; last_seen_s = -1.0 sentinel when never heartbeated
decisions.choice: app.arduino_last_heartbeat = 0.0 at startup; status route returns last_seen_s = -1.0 when last_hb == 0
decisions.rationale: Float init means arithmetic is always safe (no None-guard); -1.0 sentinel is clearly distinguishable from a real elapsed time and from zero-as-elapsed-zero; consumers should use the `online` field as the authoritative signal

decisions.decision: WiFi offline does not fight serial connection in updateWifiBadge()
decisions.choice: updateWifiBadge() checks serial statusIndicator class before clearing arduinoConnected on WiFi offline
decisions.rationale: Serial connection is the stronger signal — if the cashier has a USB cable plugged in, a WiFi dropout should not disable the Pay Now button; WiFi path only clears arduinoConnected when serial is also not connected

decisions.decision: Heartbeat timer block placed before NFC early-exit in loop()
decisions.choice: 4-line heartbeat block inserted after handleIncomingSerial() and before if (!found) return at line 530
decisions.rationale: Heartbeat must fire during idle periods when no card is in range; placement after the early-exit would prevent powerbank keep-alive and badge updates during quiet periods between taps

decisions.decision: APDU inDataExchange wrapped in retry loop
decisions.choice: Up to APDU_MAX_RETRIES=3 attempts; APDU_RETRY_DELAY_MS=150ms between attempts; responseLength reset to 60 before each; break on first success
decisions.rationale: Single inDataExchange call fails when Android HCE service isn't fully initialized after RF field cycle; ok=NO rspLen=60 confirmed in Serial Monitor; retry handles phone-to-phone HCE startup latency variation without over-penalizing fast phones with a fixed long delay
decisions.revisable: Yes — increase APDU_MAX_RETRIES or APDU_RETRY_DELAY_MS if a specific phone model still fails

decisions.decision: complete_sale_nfc Money Accounts lookup uses direct string comparison (aligning to D032)
decisions.choice: Change normalize_card_uid() comparison to str(...).strip() == money_card_number
decisions.rationale: D032 decision was direct string; implementation in S02 deviated to normalize; symmetric normalization should work but aligning to D032 and api_server.py nfc_pay() reference removes the deviation and any edge-case risk

decisions.decision: APDU exchange removed; phone payments use hardware UID via VirtualCards.PhoneUID lookup
decisions.choice: Remove APDU block from firmware — all 4-byte cards deliver CARD
decisions.rationale: uid regardless of SAK; complete_sale() falls back to VirtualCards.PhoneUID when UID not in Money Accounts; admin registers phone UIDs via POST /api/phone/register-uid
decisions.revisable: requireDeviceUnlock="true" + isPaymentAuthorized gate in Android HCE service mean the HCE only responds when screen is unlocked AND student has done biometric auth in the app — too much friction for a canteen queue; rspLen=60 (zero bytes) confirmed on real tap even with 3 retries; phone hardware UID is stable for a given device (0x08-prefix random-but-fixed-at-first-use); UID approach has zero tap friction

decisions.decision: Re-enable APDU token path; keep UID fallback auxiliary
decisions.choice: Restore firmware APDU retry loop (D037) and set Android HCE requireDeviceUnlock=false + token-only APDU response (remove biometric gate in handleSelectCommand); retain VirtualCards.PhoneUID path as optional fallback
decisions.rationale: D039 invalidated by runtime evidence: same phone produced different 0x08-prefixed UIDs (0869016D then 08E13C89), so hardware UID is not a stable primary key; token APDU is the durable identifier across taps/devices. HCE silence root cause was policy gates, not AID mismatch.
decisions.revisable: Yes — if future Android policy changes require reintroducing stricter in-app auth gates with acceptable cashier UX

decisions.decision: PN532 → RC522 on R4; phone NFC/HCE payment retired entirely
decisions.choice: RC522 RFID-only on both Arduinos; QR code payment replaces phone NFC
decisions.rationale: PN532 APDU timing issues (M003/M004) are a hardware-level liability; RC522 is already proven on R3 and handles all payment-relevant cards; QR payment is simpler, works on Android + iOS, and has no RF proximity requirement

decisions.decision: LCD → SSD1306 OLED on R4; LCD removed entirely
decisions.choice: Adafruit SSD1306 (128×64) + Adafruit GFX via hardware I2C (Wire.h) on SDA/SCL
decisions.rationale: 16×2 character LCD cannot render a QR bitmap; OLED is mandatory for QR display; Adafruit SSD1306 + GFX is the standard Arduino OLED stack with drawPixel() support needed for QR rendering

decisions.decision: QR delivery via Arduino polling; no push from server
decisions.choice: Arduino polls GET /api/arduino/qr-pending every ~500ms; backend stores pending token in app.pending_qr_token (in-memory)
decisions.rationale: Consistent with existing heartbeat/WiFi pattern; avoids Arduino running an HTTP server; ~500ms display lag is acceptable; in-memory storage is fine for single-worker PythonAnywhere; token expires after 5 minutes if unclaimed
decisions.revisable: Yes — switch to WebSocket push if display latency becomes a problem

decisions.decision: QR encodes full URL; app fetches cart separately
decisions.choice: QR bitmap encodes https://<SERVER_URL>/api/qr/<token>; app calls GET /api/qr/<token> to fetch cart before showing confirmation
decisions.rationale: URL-encoded QR works with both Android ML Kit and iOS AVFoundation scanners without custom parsing; cart details are fetched server-side so token stays opaque

decisions.decision: Android QR scanning via ML Kit Barcode Scanning
decisions.choice: com.google.mlkit:barcode-scanning — firebase-bom already in deps
decisions.rationale: ML Kit is the modern Android choice; already in firebase-bom; CameraX for preview
decisions.revisable: Yes — switch to ZXing if ML Kit causes build issues

decisions.decision: iOS QR scanning via AVFoundation (no new dependency)
decisions.choice: AVCaptureMetadataOutput with AVMetadataObjectTypeQRCode
decisions.rationale: Built-in; no new package; standard SwiftUI approach via UIViewControllerRepresentable

decisions.decision: Arduino QR generation via qrcode by Richard Moore
decisions.choice: github.com/ricmoo/qrcode — generates raw QR bit matrix; render with Adafruit GFX drawPixel()
decisions.rationale: Lightweight, no dynamic allocation, works on UNO-class RAM
decisions.revisable: Yes — if RAM pressure forces a different approach

decisions.decision: display.display() called once per renderQr(), outside pixel loop
decisions.choice: Single display.display() call after all drawPixel() calls complete
decisions.rationale: Calling display.display() inside the pixel loop would flood the I2C bus with 128×64 partial-frame transfers; one flush after all pixels are set minimises I2C traffic and eliminates frame-tear artifacts

decisions.choice: OLED init failure logs Serial message and continues; firmware does not halt
decisions.rationale: RFID payment is the critical function; OLED is display-only; halting on OLED failure would break the cashier terminal over a non-critical peripheral fault

decisions.decision: QR poll guard on lastQrUrl equality check
decisions.choice: OLED only redraws when qrUrl != lastQrUrl — unchanged state skips clearDisplay+pixel loop
decisions.rationale: Without the guard, every 500ms poll would re-render identical pixels, causing continuous I2C traffic and visible flicker; guard makes QR display state-driven not timer-driven

decisions.choice: Alias to _pyjwt to avoid naming collision with any local 'jwt' variable; api_server.py keeps bare 'jwt'
decisions.rationale: web_app.py has multiple routes that use a local variable named 'jwt'; bare import would shadow it; api_server.py is clean so it keeps bare import

decisions.decision: current_app.pending_qr_token in cashier blueprint (not bare app.)
decisions.choice: cashier_routes.py writes pending_qr_token via current_app proxy; web_app.py reads directly from app
decisions.rationale: Blueprint runs inside Flask application context; bare app.pending_qr_token would be RuntimeError outside context; current_app proxy is the correct pattern for blueprint-to-app state writes

decisions.choice: Emit precedes token clear in all qr_confirm code paths
decisions.rationale: Prevents timing race where OLED polls qr-pending and receives null before cashier UI has processed the qr_payment event; emit-then-clear is the safe ordering

decisions.choice: HTTP 402 returned when student balance < cart total in qr_confirm
decisions.rationale: Distinguishes insufficient-funds from bad-request (400); S04 mobile apps can branch on status code to show a specific "insufficient balance" message rather than a generic error dialog
decisions.revisable: Yes — align to whatever HTTP convention the iOS/Android apps settle on

decisions.decision: app.pending_qr_token is in-memory only; single token system-wide
decisions.choice: Single pending_qr_token dict in Flask app object; resets on process restart; second cashier qr-generate overwrites first
decisions.rationale: Single-worker PythonAnywhere deployment; no concurrency issue; multi-station would need per-session token scoping; documented in .env.example and DEPLOY.md
decisions.revisable: Yes — if multi-cashier concurrent deployment is ever needed

decisions.decision: Cart row rendering in POS template
decisions.choice: Build cart DOM rows with `document.createElement` + `textContent` (not string `innerHTML`) for product names/prices from Sheets
decisions.rationale: Product data is operator-editable in Google Sheets; DOM API rendering prevents accidental HTML/script injection and keeps quantity control attributes explicit for automation/tests

decisions.choice: Treat both explicit 401 and redirected `/login` fetch responses as unauthenticated and hard-redirect to `/login`
decisions.rationale: Standalone cashier auth currently redirects unauthenticated API calls; checking both signals keeps frontend robust if backend later switches between redirect vs direct 401 semantics

decisions.decision: Arduino WiFi status route naming in standalone app
decisions.choice: Serve `/api/arduino/wifi-status` as canonical route and keep `/api/arduino-wifi-status` as compatibility alias with identical payload
decisions.rationale: M006 roadmap uses slash-form route while prior implementations used hyphen-form; canonical+alias removes ambiguity and prevents firmware/UI breakage during migration to port 5010
decisions.revisable: Yes — alias can be removed once all clients consistently use canonical route

decisions.decision: NFC scope for standalone cashier payment
decisions.choice: Keep `/api/complete-sale-nfc` as a supported standalone compatibility path, but do not introduce new firmware/mobile NFC mechanisms in this slice
decisions.rationale: Roadmap requires NFC-capable sale completion in standalone app, while M005 shifted primary hardware flow to RFID+QR; preserving existing backend NFC contract satisfies checkout compatibility without reopening retired hardware scope
decisions.revisable: Yes — revisit if product direction fully removes or re-expands NFC

decisions.choice: Return HTTP 402 + `balance`/`required` for insufficient funds and include `queue` metadata when falling back to offline sync
decisions.rationale: Cashier UI and future agents must distinguish operator-correctable insufficient funds from backend outages; queue metadata exposes offline state without tailing logs
decisions.revisable: Yes — adjust only if frontend error contract changes

decisions.choice: Use a frontend payment state machine that always starts with `/api/process-sale`, then branches to RFID (`card_read` → `/api/complete-sale`), QR (`/api/qr-generate` + `qr_payment` event), or NFC (`/api/complete-sale-nfc`), with `/api/cancel-sale` and queue/wifi diagnostics always available
decisions.rationale: Keeps cashier-visible states deterministic (in-progress, waiting, success, cancelled, failed), preserves S02 cart architecture, and centralizes auth-failure redirect handling via one request helper instead of ad-hoc endpoint checks
decisions.revisable: Yes — revisit if backend payment contracts change

decisions.decision: Standalone payment/QR routes use lazy helper imports
decisions.choice: Import dashboard payment helper functions inside route handlers (not module top-level) in `backend/cashier_app/routes/payment.py` and `backend/cashier_app/routes/arduino.py`
decisions.rationale: Avoids admin-dashboard import side effects during standalone app startup/tests while still reusing battle-tested money logic; improves patchability in route-contract tests
decisions.revisable: Yes — can be revisited if helper modules are later extracted into side-effect-free shared package

decisions.decision: Live proof closure requires verifier artifact with offline classification
decisions.choice: Use a deterministic S04 verifier that writes machine-readable evidence (`S04-LIVE-PROOF.json`) and treats `success:true` + `offline:true` as degraded (not closure) for `/api/products`, `/api/complete-sale`, `/api/qr/confirm`, `/api/complete-sale-nfc`
decisions.rationale: S03 contract/runtime checks proved wiring, but milestone closure remained ambiguous without non-mocked live-Sheets proof; explicit artifact + classification prevents false positives and gives repeatable audit evidence
decisions.revisable: Yes — thresholds/required flows can be adjusted if payment contracts change

decisions.choice: Probe canonical `/api/*` routes first, then fallback to `/cashier/api/*` equivalents while keeping canonical endpoint names in persisted evidence
decisions.rationale: Current runtime exposes cashier flows under `/cashier/api/*` while closure contracts reference `/api/*`; candidate probing keeps runtime checks real without losing contract alignment
decisions.revisable: Yes — remove fallback once route prefixes are fully unified

decisions.decision: Physical UAT closure bundle for standalone cashier
decisions.choice: Gate slice closure on a combined S05 evidence bundle that links S04 live-proof output with timestamped physical artifacts (RFID tap, QR confirm, heartbeat, NFC-compatible path) and request traces on `:5010`
decisions.rationale: S05 is operator/hardware proof, not new payment logic; combining machine evidence + human captures prevents false closure when routes pass but physical flow or topology assumptions fail
decisions.revisable: Yes — adjust artifact checklist if hardware/payment contracts evolve

decisions.decision: S05 verifier required-flow fusion and artifact gate
decisions.choice: Compute each required S05 flow from S04 required-phase classification + physical-check evidence, and force failure when artifact references or request-trace endpoint hits are missing
decisions.rationale: Prevents false positives where API calls pass but physical proof is absent; surfaces exact failure reasons in `overall.failure_reasons`, `physical_checks.*.failure_reasons`, and `artifacts.missing_references` for auditable closure diagnostics
decisions.revisable: Yes — revisit if required physical checks or endpoint contracts change

decisions.choice: Remove payment-method selection UI and keep QR Pay as the default/only exposed payment path
decisions.rationale: User explicitly requested no payment methods and QR-only direction for demo scope
decisions.revisable: Yes — if product direction later requires multiple payment channels

decisions.decision: Persistence model for new settings interactions
decisions.choice: Keep accent color and personal info edit local-only (on-device), with no backend profile write in this milestone
decisions.rationale: User wants those interactions fully interactive but unnecessary to persist in database for current demo
decisions.revisable: Yes — if account/profile synchronization becomes a product requirement

decisions.choice: Remove non-mapped settings/receipt actions (Privacy & Security, Tuition Auto-Pay, Campus Discounts, PDF receipt, report issue) instead of shipping placeholders
decisions.rationale: Avoids dead-end controls and prevents scope bleed from design references into non-required product behavior
decisions.revisable: Yes — if these capabilities are later promoted into scoped requirements

decisions.choice: Use restrained animation style: polished transitions/micro-interactions that must not feel "too fancy/slow" on iOS 17+
decisions.rationale: User made responsiveness and realism a primary acceptance bar for school demo

decisions.choice: Centralize semantic tokens in `UI/Theme/AppTheme.swift` + `UI/Theme/Color+Hex.swift` and expose reusable primitives in `UI/Components` (`StitchCard`, `StitchFieldStyle`, `StitchPrimaryButtonStyle`) wired via `project.pbxproj`
decisions.rationale: Downstream slices need one stable visual language contract; moving `Color(hex:)` out of feature views removes hidden coupling and contract tests now catch missing token/primitives wiring early

decisions.choice: Use a reusable `StitchTabShell` that wraps `TabView(selection:)` with native tab bar hidden and a custom tokenized bottom bar (`StitchTabItem` metadata), while keeping session-expired alert handling in `MainTabView`
decisions.rationale: Hiding native chrome inside a `TabView` preserves route/state continuity across Home/History/Budget/Settings while enabling stitch-faithful visuals via shared `AppTheme` tokens; alert semantics remain unchanged on the authenticated shell path

decisions.choice: Apply `AppTheme` + `StitchCard`/`StitchPrimaryButtonStyle` to Home and Transactions surfaces, then lock that reuse with source-contract assertions across login, shell, home, and transactions files
decisions.rationale: This prevents visual-language drift after S01 by making cross-screen primitive adoption executable and regression-detectable while preserving existing navigation/auth/runtime behavior

decisions.decision: QR scan payload compatibility and no-dead-control guard
decisions.choice: Accept both full `/api/qr/<token>` URLs and bare token payloads, and ignore duplicate scan events after leaving `.scanning` while surfacing camera permission/setup failures as actionable UI state
decisions.rationale: OLED/cashier path now emits token-only payloads and AV capture can emit duplicate frames; without dual-format parsing + gating + explicit permission error handling, Home QR CTA can appear dead in live demo

decisions.decision: Home QR success refresh continuity signal
decisions.choice: Route `QRPayView(onSuccess:)` through `HomeViewModel.refreshAfterQRSuccess(...)` with explicit log + source-contract assertions
decisions.rationale: Keeps Home balance/transactions refresh linkage inspectable and regression-testable instead of relying on inline closure behavior that is easy to break silently during UI refactors

decisions.choice: Use `rtk proxy python` substring assertions for static QR/Home marker checks in `scripts/verify-m007-s02.sh` instead of `grep -F`-based checks
decisions.rationale: Avoids cross-shell quoting/parser drift in Windows + Git Bash execution while keeping deterministic fail-fast contract enforcement for QR-only/state/camera-key invariants

decisions.choice: Implement search + filter as client-side derivation over paginated `allTransactions` state, with explicit split channels for initial-load failure vs pagination failure
decisions.rationale: Current iOS client/backend contracts do not provide reliable server-side search/filter/has_more guarantees; local derivation plus separated error channels preserves load-more continuity and prevents populated lists from being masked by pagination errors

decisions.decision: S04 execution order and state-model seam
decisions.choice: Implement S04 in behavior-contract-first order, including a dedicated `LostCardViewModel` plus explicit Budget/Lost-Card failure channels before full UI polish
decisions.rationale: Research showed silent budget catches and lost-card session-boundary ambiguity as the highest demo-break risks; stabilizing deterministic state contracts first keeps redesign work verifiable and prevents dead-end controls

decisions.choice: Use indexed line-item iteration (`Array(lineItems.enumerated())` with `id: \.index`) plus explicit fallback markers rather than name-only identity (`id: \.name`)
decisions.rationale: Receipt rows can contain duplicate item names; name-only identity risks collisions and unstable rendering. Indexed identity keeps duplicate rows deterministic and easy to assert in design-contract tests.

decisions.choice: Persist accent/personal-info locally in SettingsViewModel, resolve accent through AppTheme, and consume through shared shell/button/home surfaces (no backend writes)
decisions.rationale: Makes local persistence visible outside Settings, avoids duplicated hardcoded accent logic, and stays consistent with D071 local-only constraint for demo scope.

decisions.choice: Add `AppTheme.Motion` with primitive intents (`primaryButtonPress`, `tabSelection`, `cardSurface`), tokenized durations/curves, and a built-in `accessibilityReduceMotion` override consumed by shared primitives
decisions.rationale: Prevents timing/curve drift across primitives, keeps reduced-motion behavior explicit and testable, and avoids ad hoc animation constants before state-screen tuning in T02/T03.

decisions.decision: Final integration gate composition for M007/S07
decisions.choice: Use a phased S07 closure gate combining integration/scope source contracts, a fail-fast verifier with structured phase diagnostics, and explicit device UAT/readiness artifacts.
decisions.rationale: Final milestone acceptance is cross-boundary and human-reviewed; combining automated contracts, diagnostic phase output, and manual evidence prevents silent regressions and makes failure localization deterministic.

decisions.decision: QR-success continuity transport across Home and Transactions in S07
decisions.choice: Use a shared @AppStorage integer tick ("qr_payment_success_continuity_tick") incremented exactly once on QR success in Home and consumed in Transactions via .task(id:) with view-model dedupe guard.
decisions.rationale: A persisted tick survives tab switches and view re-creation better than transient callbacks/notifications alone, and the one-shot + tick guard prevents duplicate refresh side effects from manual-vs-auto dismiss races.

decisions.choice: Use one phased verifier (`scripts/verify-m008-s02.sh`) that enforces native-tab rollback contract plus budget/QR/login regression suites with phase-scoped guidance output.
decisions.rationale: S02 changes navigation shell but can silently break adjacent UX contracts. A single phase-logged verifier made drift localization deterministic (`s02-rollback-contract`, `budget-regression`, `qr-regression`, `login-regression`) and is now the authoritative closure signal for this slice.

decisions.decision: Rollback verifier shape for M008 S02
decisions.choice: Adopt a single phased verifier script for tab shell, budget, QR, and login regression gates.
decisions.rationale: Navigation shell work can regress nearby UX contracts; a phased script gives deterministic failure localization and one closure command.

decisions.choice: validated
decisions.rationale: S02 implemented native `TabView` root shell in MainTabView, removed stitch shell markers, and passed rollback + regression verifier phases via `scripts/verify-m008-s02.sh` and related pytest suites.

decisions.choice: Use `scripts/verify-m008-s03.sh` as the canonical S03 verifier with phased checks (`s03-home-contract`, `home-qr-continuity`) chained to `scripts/verify-m008-s02.sh` for shell/budget/QR/login regression guard coverage.
decisions.rationale: S03 changes Home structure but must preserve QR continuity and inherited S02 rollback boundaries. A chained phased verifier keeps failure localization deterministic and prevents accepting Home-only green states while shared rollback contracts regress.

decisions.choice: Adopt `scripts/verify-m008-s03.sh` phased verifier and chain `scripts/verify-m008-s02.sh` as downstream regression guard.
decisions.rationale: Home rollback in S03 can pass local checks while breaking continuity or prior rollback boundaries. Chaining phases localizes failures and enforces inherited guardrails before slice closure.

decisions.choice: Preserve the QR success continuity seam (`didConsumePresentedQRSuccess`, continuity tick increment, and refresh handoff) unchanged while simplifying only Home surface composition.
decisions.rationale: R076 continuity guards depend on this seam; isolating rollback to visual structure avoids introducing duplicate callback churn or cross-screen refresh regressions while still delivering the minimalist hero rollback.

decisions.choice: Preserve the existing QR success continuity seam while simplifying Home surface composition.
decisions.rationale: R076 continuity guards depend on the existing callback guard and continuity tick flow; keeping that seam unchanged avoids regression during UI rollback.
